heading:
Select the correct implementation of a piece of code that accepts a sentence as input and prints the number of words in it. 

A sentence is just a sequence of words with a space between consecutive words. You can assume that the sentence will not have any other punctuation marks. You can also assume that the input string will have at least one word.

Consider the following snippet of code. What should be the input to this code to get the following output?

**Output:**
```text
Student record: 
    Dept: ABC
    Name: MR FNAME LNAME
    Roll No: 999
    Library Card No: AX999
```
media_content: dept    = input()course  = input()prefix  = input()name    = input()roll_no = input()name = prefix + " " + name.upper() # one spacelib_id = dept[0] + course[0] + roll_noprint("Student record:")indent = '    ' # four spacesprint(indent + "Dept:", dept)print(indent + "Name:", name)print(indent + "Roll No:", roll_no)print(indent + "Library Card No:", lib_id)

heading: What will be the output of the following program?
media_content: class Player {    constructor(name) {        this.name = name        this.team = 'Indian Cricket Team'        this.nationality = 'Indian'    }}class Bowler extends Player {    constructor(name, wicket, average) {        super(name)        this.role = 'Bowler'        this.wicket = wicket        this.average = average    }}bumbum = new Bowler('Jasprit Bumrah', 101, 22.79)console.log(bumbum)

heading: What will be the output of the following program?
media_content: const obj = {    a: 10,    operation(x, y, n) {        return x ** n + y + this.a    },}const arr = Array()p = obj.operationarr.push(p.bind(obj, 2)(3, 2))arr.push(p.apply(obj, [2, 2, 3, 4, 5]))arr.push(p.call(obj, 2, 3, 4))console.log(arr)

heading: What will be the output of the following program?
